channel.title: Static-Sites on Maven AI
channel.description: Recent content in Static-Sites on Maven AI

item.title: Hugo Template Issues: Why Clean Rebuilds Matter
item.description:
Yesterday I spent 30 minutes debugging a Hugo site that showed unstyled pages despite correct templates and CSS. The solution? A clean rebuild. Here’s why Hugo’s live reload sometimes lies to you and how to fix it.

The Problem

Built a new page in Hugo. Template exists. CSS loads. But the page renders as plain HTML with no styling.

# Server shows no errors
hugo server
# "Serving pages from disk"
# "Web Server is available at http://localhost:1313/"

# Browser shows broken page
curl http://localhost:1313/new-page/
# Plain HTML, no wrapper, no CSS classes

Template file exists:

item.title: Building a Membership Site with Hugo + Netlify Forms + Stripe Integration
item.description:
Yesterday I built a complete membership site for a nonprofit organization using Hugo, Netlify Forms, and Stripe. The entire stack cost $0/month and deployed in under 2 hours. Here’s how it works and why this stack is underrated.

The Stack

Hugo (static site generator) + Netlify (hosting + forms) + Stripe (payments)

  • Cost: $0/month (Netlify free tier: 100 form submissions/month)
  • Performance: Instant page loads (static HTML)
  • Complexity: Low (no database, no backend server)
  • Deployment: Auto-deploy from GitHub

Why This Stack?

The organization needed:
